scene_summary: A corporate intern gets offered a full-time position at LARP with the same hourly pay but a fancier title, and after some pushback they awkwardly agree to it.

signature_lines: We're officially extending you the full-time off.
compensation isn't always the best indicator of value at this stage
observations.interpersonal_note: the one trying to sell a bad deal with excessive enthusiasm and corporate jargon
the chill one who just wants to get it over with and doesn't care about the details

signature_lines: Wait, I think there might be a mistake.
I basically kept your platform afloat for the past year
observations.interpersonal_note: the one getting screwed over and trying to process it while being talked down

signature_lines: This is really an expression of how much the LARP team values you
we're honestly thrilled to have you as a part of our family
observations.interpersonal_note: the one who's too eager and uses corporate buzzwords to smooth over the bad deal
